evals.prompt: Who were the most positive analysts covering Apple in the past year? I want to know which ones are most bullish and which firms they work for.
evals.expected_output: A plan that: (1) calls getSpeakers for Apple with speakerTypes: ['Analysts'], sortBy: 'sentiment', sortOrder: 'desc', (2) calls getSpeakerCompanies to get firm-level sentiment, (3) presents a ranked table of analysts with name, firm, sentiment score, and sentence count, (4) identifies the most bullish and most bearish analysts (top 3 and bottom 3), (5) includes citation links.

assertions.name: get_speakers_analysts_sorted_by_sentiment
assertions.description: getSpeakers is called with speakerTypes: ['Analysts'], sortBy: 'sentiment', sortOrder: 'desc'

assertions.name: get_speaker_companies_called
assertions.description: getSpeakerCompanies is called to get firm-level sentiment ranking

assertions.name: ranked_table_present
assertions.description: Response includes a ranked table with analyst name, firm, sentiment score, and sentence count

assertions.name: top_and_bottom_3_identified
assertions.description: Most bullish (top 3) and most bearish (bottom 3) analysts are explicitly identified

assertions.name: sentiment_range_minus1_to_plus1
assertions.description: Sentiment scores are shown as numbers between -1 and 1

evals.prompt: Compare the CEO and CFO sentiment for Tesla. Who is more bullish between the two?
evals.expected_output: A plan that: (1) calls getSpeakers for Tesla with speakerTypes: ['Executives_CEO'], (2) calls getSpeakers for Tesla with speakerTypes: ['Executives_CFO'] in parallel, (3) compares their individual sentiment scores and states explicitly who is MORE BULLISH or MORE CAUTIOUS, (4) includes supporting quotes from each executive.

assertions.name: ceo_speaker_call
assertions.description: getSpeakers is called with speakerTypes: ['Executives_CEO'] for Tesla

assertions.name: cfo_speaker_call
assertions.description: getSpeakers is called with speakerTypes: ['Executives_CFO'] for Tesla

assertions.name: parallel_execution
assertions.description: CEO and CFO calls are run in parallel (same batch)

assertions.name: explicit_bullish_comparison
assertions.description: Response explicitly states who is MORE BULLISH or MORE CAUTIOUS with their sentiment scores

assertions.name: supporting_quotes_present
assertions.description: At least one supporting quote per executive is included

evals.prompt: Which companies did the top 3 most positive analysts covering Microsoft focus on? I want to understand their broader coverage.
evals.expected_output: A plan that: (1) calls getSpeakers for Microsoft with speakerTypes: ['Analysts'], sortBy: 'sentiment', sortOrder: 'desc' to get top analysts and their speakerIds, (2) extracts the speakerIds for the top 3 analysts, (3) calls searchTopCompanies ONCE PER speakerId (3 separate calls, never batched), (4) presents which companies each analyst discussed most.

assertions.name: get_speakers_called_first
assertions.description: getSpeakers is called first to identify top analysts and their speakerIds

assertions.name: speaker_ids_extracted
assertions.description: speakerIds are extracted from getSpeakers results for the top 3 analysts

assertions.name: search_top_companies_once_per_speaker
assertions.description: searchTopCompanies is called once per speakerId — 3 separate calls, never batched

assertions.name: per_analyst_company_breakdown
assertions.description: Response shows a separate company breakdown for each of the top 3 analysts
